components.component_description: Angular-based single-page application served from frontend/dist/frontend/. Handles all user-facing UI, stores JWT tokens in localStorage, and communicates with the Express backend via REST and Socket.IO. Contains intentional client-side vulnerabilities including DOM XSS, insecure localStorage token storage, and prototype pollution.
interfaces: HTTPS REST API calls to /rest/* and /api/* endpoints
WSS Socket.IO connection for real-time chat
localStorage for JWT token and basket ID storage
Angular routing (client-side navigation, guard-based access control)
DOM manipulation (template binding, innerHTML equivalent patterns)
controls: sanitize-html@1.4.2 (outdated, used in some Angular service calls)
No CSP header (helmet.contentSecurityPolicy not configured globally)
known_vulns: JWT stored in localStorage — accessible to any XSS payload
sanitize-html@1.4.2 — outdated, XSS bypass CVEs exist
Angular Universal SSR not used — client-side only, full XSS exposure
Socket.IO client 3.1.2 — outdated (current 4.x)

components.component_description: Node.js 24 / Express 4.22.1 monolith serving REST APIs, static files, and challenge logic. Contains intentional vulnerabilities including SQL injection (login, search), JWT signing with hardcoded RSA private key (lib/insecurity.ts), eval() SSTI (userProfile.ts), SSRF (profileImageUrlUpload.ts), and 21 endpoints missing authentication middleware.
components.trust_boundaries: Public Internet / Browser Boundary | Application / Data Layer Boundary | Application / File System Boundary | Container / Host Boundary
interfaces: HTTP/HTTPS REST API on port 3000 (112 routes)
WebSocket upgrade (Socket.IO) on same port
Static file serving (Angular SPA, /ftp/, /encryptionkeys/)
Prometheus /metrics endpoint
B2B API /b2b/v2/orders (JWT protected)
Admin endpoints /rest/admin/* (unauthenticated)
Password reset flow /rest/user/reset-password
OAuth2 redirect handler
controls: express-jwt@0.1.3 isAuthorized() middleware — applied on subset of routes
express-rate-limit — only on /rest/user/reset-password and 3 other endpoints
known_secrets: RSA private key hardcoded at lib/insecurity.ts:23 — 1024-bit PKCS1 — **** (permanently compromised, in public repo)
Support user credential at routes/login.ts:61 — **** (31 chars)
known_vulns: SQL injection at routes/login.ts:34 — raw string interpolation in SELECT
SQL injection at routes/search.ts:23 — LIKE clause with criteria from req.query.q
eval(code) SSTI at routes/userProfile.ts:62 — executes arbitrary JS in Node.js context
SSRF at routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts:24 — fetch(req.body.imageUrl) no allowlist
21 endpoints missing authentication (missing_auth_suspect=true)
18 endpoints missing authorization (IDOR patterns)
express-jwt@0.1.3 — no algorithms allowlist, alg:none bypass enabled
jsonwebtoken@0.4.0 — critically outdated, CVE-2022-23529
MD5 password hashing at lib/insecurity.ts:43 — no salt
1024-bit RSA key (below NIST 2048-bit minimum)
In-memory session map — no server-side logout
Admin configuration endpoints unauthenticated
Metrics endpoint unauthenticated — app topology disclosure

components.component_description: Express route handlers responsible for file ingestion and processing: multer-based multipart upload (fileUpload.ts), profile image upload from URL (profileImageUrlUpload.ts), profile image from file (profileImageFileUpload.ts). Contains XXE via libxmljs2 with noent:true, SSRF via unchecked URL fetch, and path traversal via logfileServer/keyServer/quarantineServer.
interfaces: POST /file-upload — multipart file upload (missing auth)
POST /profile/image/file — profile image file upload (missing auth)
POST /profile/image/url — profile image URL upload SSRF (missing auth)
GET /encryptionkeys/:file — key server (public access)
GET /ftp/:file and GET /ftp/ — file server with directory listing
GET /logs/:file — log file server (user-controlled filename)
GET /ftp/quarantine/:file — quarantine file server
controls: keyServer: checks for '/' in filename to prevent path traversal (partial)
File extension allowlist for profile images (jpg/jpeg/png/svg/gif)
known_vulns: XXE at routes/fileUpload.ts:83 — libxmljs2 parseXml with noent:true (external entity resolution)
SSRF at routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts:24 — fetch(url) with no URL allowlist
Path traversal at routes/logfileServer.ts:14 — path.resolve('logs/', file) with user-controlled file
Path traversal at routes/quarantineServer.ts:14 — path.resolve('ftp/quarantine/', file)
Public key exposure at routes/keyServer.ts — serves jwt.pub (RSA public key) and premium.key without auth
Directory listing at /ftp/ via serve-index — exposes file listing
File type validation by extension only — MIME type mismatch attacks possible
POST /file-upload missing authentication — anyone can upload

components.component_description: Machine-to-machine API endpoint at POST /b2b/v2/orders protected by JWT bearer auth. Processes JSON order payloads via notevil sandbox (routes/b2bOrder.ts:23) with vm.runInContext — susceptible to sandbox escape RCE. Accepts arbitrary JavaScript expressions as orderLinesData.
interfaces: POST /b2b/v2/orders — JWT bearer required at server.ts:423
Accepts JSON body with orderLinesData field containing JavaScript expressions
vm.runInContext() with notevil sandbox for evaluating order expressions
Sequelize ORM for order data persistence
controls: JWT bearer token required (isAuthorized() middleware at server.ts:423)
notevil library as 'safe eval' (CVE-prone, sandbox escape known)
RSA RS256 JWT verification (but private key is hardcoded and public)
known_secrets: RSA private key enables forging valid B2B JWT tokens — see express-backend context
known_vulns: RCE via vm.runInContext sandbox escape at routes/b2bOrder.ts:23 — notevil is not RCE-safe
JWT bearer required but private key committed to source — authentication bypassed trivially
Prototype pollution via orderLinesData input may escape notevil sandbox
YAML injection possible if orderLinesData contains YAML-like structures
No rate limiting on B2B order endpoint
Sandbox timeout only 2000ms — DoS via slow operations within notevil budget

components.component_description: Persistence layer composed of SQLite 3 via Sequelize 6.37.3 ORM for relational user/product/order data, MarsDB 0.6.11 for in-memory NoSQL storage (chatbot/memories), and local filesystem for uploaded files, encryption keys, and FTP downloads. SQL injection paths exist at the Sequelize raw-query level (login, search). The encryption keys directory (encryptionkeys/) is served publicly.
interfaces: Sequelize 6.37.3 ORM interface — models/user.ts, models/product.ts, etc.
Raw sequelize.query() calls bypassing ORM in security-critical paths
MarsDB 0.6.11 in-memory NoSQL API for chatbot/memories
SQLite file-based storage (juice-shop.db or in-memory for tests)
Filesystem read/write for uploads/, ftp/, encryptionkeys/, logs/
serve-index directory listing for /ftp/
jwt.pub RSA public key served at /encryptionkeys/jwt.pub
controls: SQLite database — single file, no network, no auth
chgrp/chmod applied to ftp/, frontend/dist/, logs/, data/, i18n/ in Dockerfile
known_secrets: RSA private key NOT stored in database but jwt.pub is served publicly
SQLite Users table contains all user hashed passwords (MD5, no salt)
known_vulns: SQL injection sink at raw sequelize.query() in routes/login.ts and routes/search.ts
NoSQL injection potential in MarsDB queries if user input passes unvalidated
No database-level access control — single SQLite file with full read/write by app process
User passwords stored as unsalted MD5 hashes — rainbow table attack trivial
Encryption keys directory served publicly — jwt.pub enables offline JWT analysis
No at-rest encryption for SQLite database containing user PII and credentials
GDPR/data minimization concerns — data-export endpoint missing auth

components.component_description: Login, token/session issuance, password-reset and MFA handlers (lib/insecurity.ts, lib/startup/registerWebsocketEvents.ts, routes/2fa.ts, routes/authenticatedUsers.ts, …). The credential-bearing entry surface — the highest-value authentication boundary in the system.
components.trust_boundaries: No trust boundary directly tied to this component.

components.component_description: Continuous-integration / deployment workflows (.github/workflows/ci.yml, .github/workflows/codeql-analysis.yml, .github/workflows/frontend-bundle-analysis.yml, .github/workflows/lint-fixer.yml, …). Build and release automation holding repository, registry and deploy credentials, with supply-chain reach into the produced artifact.
components.trust_boundaries: No trust boundary directly tied to this component.

components.component_description: Server-side socket.io real-time channel pushing live events to connected browsers. Internet-facing WebSocket surface with its own connection auth/origin checks and message-handler trust boundary.
components.trust_boundaries: No trust boundary directly tied to this component.
